statement: Samfylkingin (Social Democrats) holds 13 Althingi seats in session 157, tied as the largest party. Samfylkingin is pro-EU and leads the current government with Kristrún Frostadóttir as Prime Minister. The party submitted Iceland's EU membership application in 2009 under PM Jóhanna Sigurðardóttir. Current MPs: Kristrún Frostadóttir (Reykjavíkurkjördæmi norður), Alma D. Möller (Suðvesturkjördæmi), Dagbjört Hákonardóttir (Reykjavíkurkjördæmi norður), Logi Einarsson (Norðausturkjördæmi), Sigmundur Ernir Rúnarsson (Reykjavíkurkjördæmi norður), Jóhann Páll Jóhannsson (Reykjavíkurkjördæmi suður), Kristján Þórður Snæbjarnarson (Reykjavíkurkjördæmi suður), Guðmundur Ari Sigurjónsson (Suðvesturkjördæmi), Þórunn Sveinbjarnardóttir (Suðvesturkjördæmi), Arna Lára Jónsdóttir (Norðvesturkjördæmi), Víðir Reynisson (Suðurkjördæmi), Ása Berglind Hjálmarsdóttir (Suðurkjördæmi), Eydís Ásbjörnsdóttir (Norðausturkjördæmi).
caveats: While Samfylkingin officially supports EU membership, individual MPs may hold nuanced views on specific accession terms.

related_entries.statement: The Althingi (Icelandic parliament) has 63 seats. As of session 157, 57 seats are held by sitting MPs. The party breakdown is: Sjálfstæðisflokkur (Independence Party) 13, Samfylkingin (Social Democrats) 13, Viðreisn (Reform Party) 11, Miðflokkurinn (Centre Party) 8, Flokkur fólksins (People's Party) 8, Framsóknarflokkur (Progressive Party) 4. The remaining 6 seats are vacant or held by alternates (varamenn).

The current Icelandic government is a coalition of Samfylkingin (Social Democrats, 13 seats), Viðreisn (Reform Party, 11 seats), and Flokkur fólksins (People's Party, 8 seats), holding 32 of 63 Althingi seats. The Prime Minister is Kristrún Frostadóttir (Samfylkingin). This is the first government coalition where both explicitly pro-EU parties (Samfylkingin and Viðreisn) serve together, which has been cited as a factor in advancing the EU referendum.

Samfylkingin (Social Democrats) and Viðreisn (Reform Party) are the two Althingi parties that formally support EU membership. Samfylkingin has supported EU membership since 2007 and led the 2009 application process while in government. Viðreisn was founded in 2016 partly on a pro-EU platform and has consistently advocated for completing accession negotiations. Together they held approximately 17 of 63 Althingi seats after the 2024 elections.

citations.report_title: 25. þáttur Silfursins: Stefnt að kosningu um ESB í sumar

claims.claim_text: Ekki var meirihluti á Alþingi fyrir ESB-aðild þegar farið var í aðildarviðræður 2009 í tíð Samfylkingar og Jóhönnu Sigurðardóttur.
